name: Franklin Savings Bank
description_short: Franklin Savings Bank is a New Hampshire-based community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, lending, and credit cards with online and mobile banking.
description_long: Franklin Savings Bank is a community-focused financial institution headquartered in New Hampshire that serves both personal and business customers. The bank has established itself as a regional player with multiple locations throughout New Hampshire and a commitment to local community involvement, as evidenced by their grants to local organizations and schools. The bank operates a traditional full-service banking model with both in-person branch locations and digital banking capabilities. The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, individual retirement accounts, and Visa debit and credit cards. They provide modern conveniences such as online and mobile banking, text alerts, Zelle for peer-to-peer transfers, and access to Allpoint ATM networks. For business customers, Franklin Savings Bank provides business checking, business savings and CDs, commercial lending through a dedicated team, business credit cards, cash management services, lockbox services, and government banking solutions. Their deposit products include rate specials on CDs and IRAs that are periodically updated. What distinguishes Franklin Savings Bank is its positioning as a community bank with active involvement in local philanthropic efforts, combined with modern banking technology. They offer identity theft resolution services and debit card monitoring, suggesting a focus on consumer protection. The bank also features Insured Cash Sweep (ICS) for business customers, which provides FDIC protection beyond standard limits. Their accessibility features and fraud prevention resources indicate attention to customer security and usability. Franklin Savings Bank operates as a traditional regional bank with solid fundamentals but lacks the extensive product depth or national brand recognition of larger institutions. The website content suggests they are a stable, community-oriented option for customers seeking personalized service in New Hampshire. However, as a regional bank, their rates and product offerings may not be competitive with online-only banks or larger national institutions in all categories.
services: Personal checking accounts with e-statements and check ordering
Certificates of deposit with periodic rate specials
Visa debit and credit cards (personal and business)
Online and mobile banking with text alerts
Business checking and business savings accounts
Commercial lending and business relationship management
Business cash management and lockbox services
Debit card monitoring and fraud prevention
pros: Multiple physical branch locations throughout New Hampshire with in-person customer service
Offers both personal and business banking with dedicated commercial lending team
Identity theft resolution services and debit card monitoring included
Access to Allpoint ATM network for convenient cash withdrawal
Insured Cash Sweep (ICS) protection for business accounts exceeding FDIC limits
Modern digital services including online banking, mobile app, and Zelle payments
Active community involvement with documented grants to local schools and nonprofits
Accessibility features on website including keyboard navigation and text resizing options
cons: As a regional bank, may offer less competitive rates than national banks or online-only institutions
Limited geographic availability—primarily serves New Hampshire only
No evidence of premium checking account features like fee waivers or higher interest rates compared to online alternatives
Fraud notices on website indicate recent fraud targeting (though this reflects industry-wide phishing issues)
Business lending and premium products likely require relationship-building and in-person interaction
best_for: New Hampshire residents seeking local community bank relationships with in-person service
Small to medium-sized businesses needing dedicated commercial lending teams and local decision-making
Customers prioritizing branch access and personalized service over maximum interest rates
Business owners requiring government banking, cash management, and lockbox services
diagnosis: Franklin Savings Bank is best for New Hampshire residents and businesses seeking a community-oriented bank with physical branch locations, personalized service, and modern digital banking capabilities. The primary caveat is that as a regional institution, they may not offer the most competitive rates compared to larger national banks or online-only competitors, and their services are geographically limited to New Hampshire.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Franklin Savings Bank offers checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, loans, and credit cards for New Hampshire customers with online and mobile banking.
